mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment weakens consumer demand and lowers inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as investors seek safe assets.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward yields rise more than 50% of the time when UNRATE increases in the top quintile of its distribution, then hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contraction in manufacturing, leading to lower bond yields due to recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: If after an INDPRO decline >2% MOM, the 20-day yield return is positive in more than 30% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and a reduction in the federal funds rate indicates a monetary stimulus supporting recovery, which pushes long-term yields upward as growth and inflation expectations improve.
failure_condition: If the combination of rising GDP (QoQ > 0.5%) and falling FEDFUNDS (down 25bp over quarter) does not produce a significantly positive 60-day yield return (Rank IC < 0.03), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a deflation threat environment, an increase in CPI breaks the deflation expectation, causing bond yields to rise as real yields adjust upward.
failure_condition: If CPI increases but the 20-day yield return is negative in more than 40% of instances during deflation regimes (CPI YoY < 1%), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising signals economic recovery and higher long-run growth, which pushes up the 10-year Treasury yield as investors demand higher compensation for future inflation.
failure_condition: If the 5-day yield change following an increase in HOUST (top quintile) is negative on average, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly payroll losses deepen recession fears and increase demand for safe government bonds, driving down yields.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS change less than -200,000 in one month
failure_condition: If 20-day yield returns are positive in more than 40% of instances after a PAYEMS decline >200K, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining unemployment signals strong consumer spending and labor market tightening, which increases inflation expectations and pushes bond yields higher.
failure_condition: If when both RSAFS increases and UNRATE decreases, the 20-day yield return is not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-financial crisis recovery regime, increasing real GDP validates the recovery narrative, leading to higher yields as risk appetite improves and safe-haven demand wanes.
failure_condition: If during the first 12 months post-crisis, rising GDPC1 (quarterly growth > 1%) does not produce a significantly positive 60-day yield return, reject.
